'''Daniel Heckenberg''' (born 27 October 1979) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international rugby league footballer. He played as a {{rlp|pr}} at club level in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], in the [[Super League]] for [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], and in the [[RFL Championship]] for the [[Widnes Vikings]].

==Early career==
Heckenberg was born in [[Camden, New South Wales, Australia]].

Heckenberg began his rugby career at [[St Gregory's College, Campbelltown]], a school renowned for producing young [[rugby league]] footballers. He first played internationally as part of the [[Australian Schoolboys]] team in a match against the [[British Amateur Rugby League Association|BARLA Young Lions]]. After his graduation he played for a short while as an amateur for the [[Eagle Vale, New South Wales|Eagle Vale]]-[[St Andrews, New South Wales|St Andrews]] junior side.<ref name=Guide08>{{cite web|title=Harlequins RL Official Club Guide 2008 - Player Profiles|publisher=[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]|year=2008|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/uploads/documents/ClubGuide2008.pdf|accessdate=10 September 2008|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20081031014654/http://www.quins.co.uk/uploads/documents/ClubGuide2008.pdf|archivedate=31 October 2008 }}</ref>

==NRL==
He began his professional career at [[National Rugby League]] side [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], progressing through the youth team and earning his first grade début in 2000. In the same year, Heckenberg made his first appearance as a Scotland international, participating in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]. He moved club in 2002, joining the [[Parramatta Eels]], before changing team again in 2004, this time playing with the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. He provided much defensive support for the Sea Eagles and was held in high esteem by his peers at the club.<ref name=Guide08/>

==Harlequins Rugby League==
His performances led to a transfer to Super League side [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins]], and he soon became an important part of the team's defensive game. Heckenberg confirmed his first grade place in the beginning of his second year at the club but suffered a severe shoulder injury in a match against [[Bradford Bulls]] in May, bringing his 2007 [[Super League XII]] campaign to an end.<ref name=Guide08/><ref name="QUINS SNAP UP BANNISTER">{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/may/28/rugbyleague.sport1?commentpage=1|title=Quins fall for Vainikolo's trick|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=28 May 2007|accessdate=10 September 2008| location=London| first=Gareth| last=Walker}}</ref> Harlequins coach [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] described Heckenberg as a hard worker and an integral part of the team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37452&newstype=n&matchid=&Refid=&storytype=RL|title=Heckenberg out with shoulder injury|publisher=[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]|date=13 June 2007|accessdate=13 June 2007|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070928015606/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37452&newstype=n&matchid=&Refid=&storytype=RL|archivedate=28 September 2007 }}</ref> Heckenberg underwent surgery in June to correct the problem but when he returned from injury for 2008's [[Super League XIII]] he again suffered a setback. He dislocated his shoulder in training in March 2008, resulting in a six-week absence from the team.<ref>{{cite news|title=Fresh injury blow for Harlequins|work=[[BBC Sport]]|date=18 March 2008|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/7298161.stm|access-date=10 September 2008}}</ref>

==Representative==
Heckenberg was named in the [[List of Scotland national rugby league team players#Players|Scotland training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] but was forced to withdraw from the competition due to injury.<ref name="Henderson trio in Scots cup squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7587971.stm|title=Henderson trio in Scots cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=29 August 2008|access-date=3 October 2008}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Teams - Scotland|publisher=[[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] Official site|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/teams/Scotland2.aspx|accessdate=10 September 2008|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080915194603/http://www.rlwc08.com/teams/Scotland2.aspx|archivedate=15 September 2008}}</ref><ref name="Heckenberg out of the World Cup">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=39548&newstype=n&matchid=&Refid=&storytype=RL|title=Heckenberg out of the World Cup|publisher=Quins|date=29 September 2008|accessdate=3 October 2008}}{{dead link|date=September 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

==Personal==
In his spare time, Heckenberg enjoys listening to the music of the [[Foo Fighters]] and [[U2]], and also helps to train youth rugby league players at Elmbridge Rugby League Club.<ref name=Guide08/>
